summary: Do not ship this plan yet. P-1 and P-2 are resolved, and the RED record matches f4497d2 and the exact firing symptom. P-3 remains broken. No materially simpler safe fix than the left-side rollup was identified.

findings.title: P-3 characterization cannot accept or reliably validate the planned rule
findings.body: `$R` is a ConfigMap whose rule YAML is embedded under `.data["r6.yaml"]`; it has no `.spec.groups`, so the proposed extraction returns an empty `EXPR` and the positive greps fail. After correcting that path, the broad negative pattern also matches the required P-1 RHS guard's legitimate `max by (app) (`. Finally, bare `! grep` is a middle negation that this repository explicitly detects as a Bats false-green. Following the plan therefore leaves characterization either permanently red or unable to enforce the intended negative contract; removing the RHS guard to satisfy it would reintroduce P-1.
findings.recommendation: First extract `.data["r6.yaml"]`, parse its `.groups[].rules[]`, and require exactly one non-empty record expression. Use `run grep ...; [ "$status" -ne 0 ]` and narrow the negative assertion to a broken `max by (app)` specifically around `ghcr_latest_digest`, so the RHS existence guard remains allowed.

next_steps: Human-triage this residual round-2 finding; the plan gate remains blocked.
Correct the P-3 assertions without removing or weakening the RHS existence guard.
